items.title: N-Vision-74 Documentation
items.text: Welcome to the N-Vision-74 project documentation. This comprehensive guide covers everything you need to know about building and customizing your 1/10 scale vehicle.

items.title: Project Overview
items.text:
N-Vision-74 is a sophisticated 1/10 scale vehicle platform featuring:

  • Modular Assembly Structure - Easy-to-assemble components with clear part organization
  • Detailed CAD Models - Professional SolidWorks files for each component
  • 3D Component Previews - Interactive visualization of all major assemblies
  • Complete Documentation - From BOM to assembly guides and technical specs

items.title: Getting Started
items.text:
• Bill of Materials - Complete parts list with specifications
  • Cost Analysis - Budget breakdown and cost optimization
  • Parts Inventory - Detailed parts catalog

items.title: Technical Documentation
items.text:
• Quick Start Guide - Get up to speed quickly
  • Assembly Guide - Step-by-step assembly instructions

items.title: 3D Component Previews
items.text: Explore interactive 3D models of all major components: - Body & Shell - Chassis - Suspension - Drivetrain - Electronics - Steering

items.title: Resources
items.text:

  • CAD Files - Source files and models

items.title: Budget Reduction Strategies
items.text:
1. Selective 3D Printing ($20-50 savings)
  2. Print non-critical parts yourself

  3. Use cheaper plastic materials

  4. Bulk Fastener Purchases ($10-15 savings)

  5. Buy assorted fastener kits

  6. Lower per-unit costs

  7. Alternative Materials ($30-60 savings)

  8. Use aluminum instead of steel where possible

  9. Standard plastic vs. carbon fiber bracing

  10. Used Components ($50-100 savings)

  11. Source used motors from previous builds
  12. Refurbished electronics

items.title: Premium Build Upgrade ($200-300 additional)
items.text:
• Carbon fiber chassis ($80)
  • High-performance motor ($150)
  • Ball-bearing differentials ($50)
  • Titanium fasteners ($30+)

items.text:

  6. Body & Shell - Final enclosure
  7. Accessories - Mirrors, lights, decals

items.text:

items.title: Chassis Components
items.text: Interactive 3D preview of the N-Vision-74 chassis assembly.

items.title: Overview
items.text: The chassis is the structural backbone of the vehicle, providing mounting points for all major systems including suspension, drivetrain, and electronics.

items.title: Main Chassis Plate
items.text:
The primary structural member that integrates all subsystems.

Part Number: 04_Chassis_Main_Plate.SLDPRT Material: Aluminum 6061 (3mm) Weight: ~200g Dimensions: 260mm L × 140mm W × 3mm H

items.title: Features
items.text:
• CNC-machined aluminum construction
  • Precision-drilled mounting holes
  • Integrated cable routing channels
  • Reinforced corner sections
  • Lightweight yet rigid design
  • Anti-corrosion anodized finish

items.title: Front Module
items.text:
The front structural section that provides suspension and steering mounting points.

Part Number: 05_Chassis_Front_Module.SLDPRT Material: Aluminum 6061 Weight: ~80g Mounting Points: 6

items.title: Features
items.text:
• Integrated steering tower mount
  • Front suspension attachment points
  • Battery tray mounting surface
  • Electronics enclosure support
  • Aerodynamic fairing integration

items.title: Rear Module
items.text:
The rear structural section for rear suspension and drivetrain mounting.

Part Number: 06_Chassis_Rear_Module.SLDPRT Material: Aluminum 6061 Weight: ~80g Mounting Points: 6

items.title: Features
items.text:
• Rear suspension tower mount
  • Motor/differential mounting
  • Rear light housing integration
  • Electronics cooling provisions
  • Symmetrical design for balance

items.title: Step 1: Prepare Main Plate
items.text:
• Inspect for damage
  • Debur all edges
  • Clean with compressed air
  • Place on flat work surface

items.title: Step 2: Install Front Module
items.text:
1. Position front module at front of main plate
  2. Align all mounting holes
  3. Insert M4 socket head screws
  4. Tighten in X-pattern (cross pattern)
  5. Torque to 3-4 Nm

items.title: Step 3: Install Rear Module
items.text:
1. Position rear module at rear of main plate
  2. Align with reference marks
  3. Insert M4 screws with lock washers
  4. Tighten in X-pattern
  5. Torque to 3-4 Nm

items.title: Socket Head Cap Screws
items.text: Size Location Quantity Torque M3 Bracing 8 1.5-2.0 Nm M4 Module mount 12 3-4 Nm M5 Tower mount 8 5-6 Nm

items.title: Lock Washers & Nuts
items.text:
• Use lock washers under all screw heads
  • Use flanged nuts for open holes
  • Flanged nut size: M4 (minimum 10 pieces)

items.title: Weight Specifications
items.text: Component Weight Percentage Main Plate 200g 40% Front Module 80g 16% Rear Module 80g 16% Fasteners 40g 8% Bracing 100g 20% Total 500g 100%

items.title: Front Module Mounting Points
items.text:
• Steering tower: Center top
  • Front suspension: 4 points
  • Battery tray: Side mounting
  • Electronics box: Top mounting

items.title: Rear Module Mounting Points
items.text:
• Rear suspension: 4 points
  • Motor mount: Center
  • Differential housing: Center-bottom
  • Electronics: Optional rear

items.title: Common Upgrades
items.text:
1. Carbon Fiber Bracing
  2. Reduces weight by 20-30%
  3. Improves rigidity

  4. Cost: $30-50 additional

  5. Aluminum Bracing Upgrade

  6. More durable than carbon fiber
  7. Better for harsh conditions

  8. Cost: $15-25 additional

  9. Custom Electronics Mount

  10. Move ESC/receiver location
  11. Requires custom drilling

  12. Improves weight distribution

  13. Ballast Mount

  14. Add adjustable weight system
  15. Fine-tune balance and handling
  16. Cost: $20-40

items.title: Performance Considerations
items.text: Modification Effect Difficulty Carbon Fiber Bracing +15% stiffness, -20% weight Medium Aluminum Upgrade +30% stiffness Low Electronics Relocation Better balance Medium Ballast System Tunable handling High

items.title: After Each Run
items.text:
• Inspect for cracks
  • Check fastener tightness
  • Clean mud and debris
  • Dry thoroughly

items.title: Weekly (Regular Use)
items.text:
• Detailed fastener inspection
  • Check alignment (straight line test)
  • Inspect welded/brazed joints
  • Test flexing by hand

items.title: Monthly
items.text:
• Re-torque all fasteners
  • Deep clean
  • Inspect for fatigue cracks
  • Check for corrosion

items.title: Storage Best Practices
items.text:
• Store in dry environment
  • Avoid direct sunlight (UV damage)
  • Protect from moisture
  • Keep fasteners in sealed container

items.title: Corrosion Prevention
items.text:
• Use stainless steel fasteners
  • Apply anodized coating if damaged
  • Store with desiccant packets
  • Check for oxidation regularly

items.title: Drivetrain Components
items.text: Interactive 3D preview of the N-Vision-74 drivetrain system.

items.title: Overview
items.text: The drivetrain transfers power from the motor to all wheels through a center differential system.

items.title: Center Differential Assembly
items.text:
The differential distributes torque to front and rear axles.

Part Number: Center_diff_top.SLDPRT / Center_diff_bottom.SLDPRT Material: Aluminum Weight: ~60g (assembled)

items.title: Main Components
items.text:
• Differential housing (top and bottom)
  • Helical gear set
  • Cross-pin and shafts
  • Side gears for axle drive

items.title: Differential Specifications
items.text: Specification Value Type Open differential (helical) Gear Ratio 3.73:1 standard Max Torque 80 Nm Oil Capacity 10ml Temperature Limit 80°C

items.title: Helical Gear Design
items.text:
• Low noise operation
  • Smooth power delivery
  • Torque split between axles
  • Optional locking mechanism

items.title: Differential Covers
items.text:
Top Cover Part: Center_diff_top_cover.SLDPRT Material: Polycarbonate Weight: ~15g

Bottom Cover Part: Center_diff_bottom.SLDPRT Material: Aluminum Weight: ~20g

Features: - Dust sealing - Easy access for maintenance - Oil containment - Bearing support

items.title: Motor Mount System
items.text: Part Number: Motor_support_mount_1.SLDPRT / Motor_support_mount_2.SLDPRT / Motor_support_mount_3.SLDPRT Material: Aluminum Weight: ~35g (total)

items.title: Mount Configuration
items.text:
• 3-point adjustable system
  • Allows pinion gear adjustment
  • Supports motor load
  • Vibration damping

items.title: Alignment Procedure
items.text:
1. Loosen all motor mount bolts
  2. Position motor for smooth mesh
  3. Check pinion-to-ring clearance (0.5mm)
  4. Tighten progressively
  5. Test rotation for binding

items.title: Battery Safety
items.text:

  • Never overcharge (4.25V per cell max)

items.title: Ackermann Steering Principle
items.text: The inside wheel turns more sharply than the outside wheel for optimal cornering.

items.title: Steering Angles
items.text: Parameter Specification Total Steering Travel 90° (45° each direction) Inside Wheel Lock 35° Outside Wheel Lock 25° Toe Adjustment -4° to +4° Camber Adjustment -3° to +3°

items.title: Toe-In Adjustment
items.text:
Purpose: Wheel alignment parallel to vehicle centerline

Procedure: 1. Place vehicle on flat surface 2. Measure distance between wheel fronts (A) 3. Measure distance between wheel rears (B) 4. Front should be 2-4mm closer (A < B) 5. Adjust tie rod length to achieve toe

items.title: Servo Center Calibration
items.text:
1. Power on ESC with throttle centered
  2. Note servo position
  3. Move steering trim to center
  4. Servo should be mechanically centered
  5. Adjust trim as needed

items.title: Endpoint Adjustment
items.text:
1. Steer full left
  2. Measure wheel lock angle
  3. Adjust servo endpoint if needed
  4. Repeat for right side
  5. Balance left and right travel

items.text: Setting Effect Use Case Standard 1:1 input ratio General driving Sensitive 1:0.8 ratio Technical terrain Dampened 1:1.2 ratio High-speed racing

items.title: Under-Steer (Vehicle Won't Turn)
items.text:
Causes: - Tie rods too long - Toe-in excessive - Servo endpoint limited

Solutions: 1. Shorten front tie rods 2. Reduce toe-in 3. Increase servo endpoints 4. Verify servo power

items.title: Over-Steer (Vehicle Over-Responds)
items.text:
Causes: - Tie rods too short - Negative toe-out - Excessive servo sensitivity

Solutions: 1. Lengthen front tie rods 2. Adjust toe toward neutral 3. Reduce servo sensitivity 4. Check for loose joints

items.title: Pulling to One Side
items.text:
Causes: - Unequal servo throw - Bent tie rod - Misaligned hub

Solutions: 1. Balance steering trim 2. Verify tie rod straightness 3. Check wheel alignment 4. Test on different surface

  • Check for looseness

items.text:

  • Check servo gears for wear

  2. Servo Curve - Non-linear response programming
  3. Dual Rate - Different sensitivity per speed
  4. Exponential - Smoother low-speed control

items.title: Soft Suspension

items.title: Detailed Assembly Guide
items.text: Step-by-step instructions for assembling your N-Vision-74 vehicle.

items.title: Step 1.1: Prepare Main Plate
items.text:
1. Retrieve main chassis plate (Part 04)
  2. Inspect for damage or manufacturing defects
  3. Debur all edges with a file
  4. Clean with compressed air
  5. Place on flat, stable work surface

items.title: Step 1.2: Install Front Module
items.text:
1. Position front module (Part 05) at front of main plate
  2. Align mounting holes precisely
  3. Insert M4 socket head screws into corner brackets
  4. Tighten sequentially in X-pattern to prevent warping
  5. Torque spec: M4 = 3-4 Nm
  6. Verify alignment with calipers

items.title: Step 1.3: Install Rear Module
items.text:
1. Position rear module (Part 06) at rear of main plate
  2. Align with reference marks on main plate
  3. Insert M4 screws with lock washers
  4. Tighten in X-pattern
  5. Torque spec: M4 = 3-4 Nm
  6. Ensure perpendicular alignment

items.title: Step 1.4: Add Cross Braces
items.text:
1. Position carbon fiber brace on top surface
  2. Mark mounting hole locations
  3. Drill if necessary (use pilot holes first)
  4. Install with M3 screws and lock washers
  5. Torque spec: M3 = 1.5-2 Nm
  6. Check for symmetry

items.title: Step 1.5: Final Chassis Check
items.text:
• All fasteners tight
  • No visible gaps at joints
  • Chassis sits flat on table
  • No warping or twisting

Completion Time: 45-60 minutes

items.title: Step 2.1: Install Front Tower
items.text:
1. Position front tower assembly at front of chassis
  2. Align dowel pins with mounting holes
  3. Insert M5 socket head screws (4 total)
  4. Tighten gradually to proper torque
  5. Torque spec: M5 = 5-6 Nm

items.title: Step 2.2: Mount Front Suspension Arms
items.text:
1. Insert top arm pivots into tower mounts
  2. Secure with M4 socket head screws
  3. Install bottom arm pivots
  4. Add lock washers under all screw heads
  5. Torque spec: M4 = 3-4 Nm
  6. Verify smooth pivoting action

items.title: Step 2.3: Install Shock Absorbers
items.text:
1. Position shock absorber (front-left)
  2. Mount upper ball joint to tower arm mount
  3. Mount lower ball joint to chassis bracket
  4. Repeat for front-right shock
  5. Test compression and rebound smoothly

Front Suspension Time: 1.5-2 hours

items.title: Step 2.4: Install Rear Tower
items.text:
1. Position rear tower assembly at rear of chassis
  2. Align with mounting plate
  3. Insert M5 socket head screws (4 total)
  4. Tighten to proper torque
  5. Torque spec: M5 = 5-6 Nm

items.title: Step 2.5: Mount Rear Suspension Arms
items.text:
1. Insert arm pivots into tower
  2. Secure with M4 socket head screws
  3. Add lock washers
  4. Torque spec: M4 = 3-4 Nm
  5. Verify movement range

items.title: Step 2.6: Install Rear Shocks
items.text:
1. Position shock absorber (rear-left)
  2. Mount to arm and chassis brackets
  3. Repeat for rear-right shock
  4. Test full suspension movement
  5. Check for rubbing or interference

Rear Suspension Time: 1.5-2 hours

items.title: Step 3.1: Prepare Differential
items.text:
1. Remove differential from package
  2. Inspect all gears and bearings
  3. Clean with light oil if stored
  4. Check for smooth rotation

items.title: Step 3.2: Install Motor
items.text:
1. Position brushless motor on motor mounts
  2. Align with differential shaft
  3. Secure with M3 socket head screws (4 total)
  4. Torque spec: M3 = 1.5-2 Nm
  5. Check alignment - no rubbing

items.title: Step 3.3: Connect Transmission
items.text:
1. Align motor pinion with differential ring gear
  2. Install motor-to-diff connector
  3. Test rotation - should be smooth
  4. Check gear mesh - no binding

items.title: Step 3.4: Install Differential Housing
items.text:
1. Position differential unit in chassis center
  2. Align mounting holes with chassis
  3. Install mounting brackets
  4. Secure with M4 socket head screws
  5. Torque spec: M4 = 3-4 Nm

items.title: Step 3.5: Add Support Braces
items.text:
1. Install center brace holders
  2. Mount braces to prevent deflection
  3. Verify no interference with moving parts

Drivetrain Assembly Time: 1.5-2 hours

items.title: Step 4.1: Prepare Electronics Enclosure
items.text:
1. Mount battery trays (L/R) on chassis sides
  2. Secure with M3 screws
  3. Install ESC in weatherproof enclosure
  4. Mount enclosure on top of chassis

items.title: Step 4.2: Motor Connection
items.text:
1. Connect motor leads to ESC:
  2. Red = Positive
  3. Black = Negative
  4. Connection type depends on ESC
  5. Solder connections with rosin core solder
  6. Apply heatshrink tubing over joints
  7. Test continuity with multimeter

items.title: Step 4.3: Servo Installation
items.text:
1. Mount servo in servo bracket
  2. Secure with servo clips/screws
  3. Connect servo cable to ESC receiver channel 1
  4. Run cable along chassis frame

items.title: Step 4.4: Battery Mounting
items.text:
1. Place LiPo battery in battery trays
  2. Secure with velcro or straps
  3. Ensure battery is centered for weight balance
  4. Connect to ESC battery connector

items.title: Step 4.5: Final Electronics Check
items.text:
• All connections seated properly
  • No exposed wires or connectors
  • Servo moves when powered
  • Motor responds to throttle
  • Cooling fan works

Electronics Installation Time: 1-1.5 hours

items.title: Step 5.1: Mount Servo Arm
items.text:
1. Install servo arm on servo shaft
  2. Tighten servo arm screw securely
  3. Servo should be centered position
  4. Mark neutral position with tape

items.title: Step 5.2: Connect Steering Linkage
items.text:
1. Connect servo arm to steering tower
  2. Use ball end links for pivot points
  3. Adjust length for neutral steering
  4. Test full steering range

items.title: Step 5.3: Tie Rod Setup
items.text:
1. Mount tie rods front and rear
  2. Adjust lengths for toe-in alignment
  3. Set to manufacturer specifications
  4. Tighten lock nuts carefully

items.title: Step 5.4: Test Steering Response
items.text:
1. Center steering with transmitter
  2. Verify vehicle steers straight
  3. Test full left turn
  4. Test full right turn
  5. Check for binding or resistance

Steering Assembly Time: 45-60 minutes

items.title: Step 6.1: Install Main Body Shell
items.text:
1. Position body shell on chassis
  2. Align with mounting posts
  3. Secure with body clips or fasteners
  4. Ensure no interference with moving parts

items.title: Step 6.2: Attach Bumpers
items.text:
1. Position front bumper (Part 02)
  2. Secure with provided fasteners
  3. Install rear bumper (Part 03)
  4. Check alignment and clearance

items.title: Step 6.3: Add Accessories
items.text:
1. Install mirrors (Parts 09, 10)
  2. Mount light housings (Part 11)
  3. Install interior tub (Part 12)
  4. Add any custom decals or graphics

items.title: Step 6.4: Final Inspection
items.text:
• All body parts secure
  • No sharp edges exposed
  • Wheels turn freely
  • Vehicle is symmetrical
  • All lights/mirrors functional

Body Installation Time: 1-1.5 hours

items.title: Pre-Run Inspection
items.text: System Check Status Chassis All fasteners tight ✓ Suspension Arms move freely ✓ Drivetrain Smooth rotation ✓ Electronics All connections secure ✓ Steering Full range of motion ✓ Body All parts secure ✓

items.title: Torque Specifications Reference
items.text: Fastener Torque M3 1.5-2 Nm M4 3-4 Nm M5 5-6 Nm M6 8-10 Nm

items.title: General Guidelines
items.text:
• ✓ Work in clean, organized workspace
  • ✓ Organize screws by size/function
  • ✓ Take photos during disassembly for reference
  • ✓ Use calibrated torque wrench for precision
  • ✓ Never force parts - check alignment first
  • ✓ Allow solder joints to cool before moving

items.title: Common Mistakes to Avoid
items.text:
• ✗ Over-tightening fasteners (causes stripping)
  • ✗ Skipping lock washers (fasteners loosen)
  • ✗ Incorrect servo orientation (reversed steering)
  • ✗ Poor motor-diff alignment (binding/noise)
  • ✗ Neglecting threadlock (fasteners fall out)

items.title: Quality Control
items.text:
After assembly completion:

  1. Visual Inspection - Check all joints, no gaps
  2. Movement Test - Suspension, steering, wheels
  3. Electrical Test - Power, motor, servo responses
  4. Torque Audit - Re-check all fasteners
  5. Run-through - Slow, careful test drive

Total Assembly Time: 8-12 hours (depends on experience) Difficulty: Intermediate Last Updated: 2026-07-05

items.title: Phase 1: Chassis Foundation
items.text:

  • Verify all dimensions against CAD
  • Clean and deburr aluminum edges
  • Assemble main plate with braces
  • Install corner brackets
  • Verify squareness and flatness

Estimated Time: 1-2 hours

items.text:

  • Mount front suspension arms
  • Install rear tower assembly
  • Mount rear suspension arms
  • Install shock absorbers (4 total)

Estimated Time: 2-3 hours

items.title: Phase 3: Drivetrain Assembly
items.text:
• Assemble differential unit
  • Install center differential
  • Mount motor supports
  • Attach transmission components
  • Install drivetrain braces
  • Check for smooth rotation

Estimated Time: 1.5-2 hours

items.title: Phase 4: Electronics Setup
items.text:
• Mount battery trays
  • Install ESC in enclosure
  • Mount servo with brackets
  • Connect motor to ESC
  • Mount fan shroud for cooling
  • Test all electrical connections

Estimated Time: 1-2 hours

items.title: Phase 5: Steering Assembly
items.text:
• Install steering tower
  • Mount servo arm
  • Connect steering linkages
  • Adjust tie rod lengths
  • Center steering neutral position
  • Test full steering range

Estimated Time: 1 hour

items.title: Phase 6: Body & Trim
items.text:
• Position main shell on chassis
  • Install front bumper
  • Install rear bumper
  • Add mirrors and accessories
  • Apply decals and graphics
  • Final visual inspection

Estimated Time: 1-2 hours

items.title: Pre-Run Checklist
items.text:
• All fasteners tight (check with hex key)
  • Suspension arms move freely
  • Steering responds smoothly
  • Motor spins freely without load
  • Battery fully charged
  • ESC programmed correctly
  • Transmitter paired with receiver

items.title: Initial Setup Steps
items.text:
1. Calibrate Servo Center 

    1. Power on ESC with throttle stick centered
2. Steering should be centered
3. Adjust trim if needed

  2. Test Motor Direction 

    1. Connect battery
2. Slowly increase throttle
3. Motor should spin smoothly
4. Reverse throttle to stop

  3. Brake Testing 

    1. Apply gentle brake
2. Vehicle should stop smoothly
3. Check wheel alignment after stop

  4. Steering Test 

    1. Center steering
2. Turn full left - measure angle
3. Turn full right - measure angle
4. Angles should be equal

items.title: After First Run
items.text:
• Inspect all fasteners
  • Check for cracks or damage
  • Verify wheel alignment
  • Clean out dust and debris

items.title: Weekly (Regular Use)
items.text:
• Clean exterior body
  • Check tire wear and pressure
  • Inspect suspension arms
  • Test steering response

items.title: Monthly
items.text:
• Deep clean electronics
  • Lubricate suspension joints
  • Check differential fluid level
  • Test battery voltage
  • Inspect for cracks/damage

items.title: Before Events/Competition
items.text:
• Full system inspection
  • Replace worn tires if needed
  • Fresh bearing grease
  • Test all electronics
  • Verify fastener torque specs

items.title: Vehicle Won't Move
items.text:
1. Check battery voltage (should be 7.2V+)
  2. Verify motor connections
  3. Test ESC power light
  4. Check for mechanical blockage

items.title: Steering Not Responding
items.text:
1. Check servo power connection
  2. Verify transmitter is on
  3. Check servo connector seating
  4. Test servo manually

items.title: Suspension Feels Stiff
items.text:
1. Check shock oil level
  2. Look for dirt in joints
  3. Verify fastener tightness
  4. Test arm movement manually

items.title: Overheating Issues
items.text:
1. Check fan shroud placement
  2. Verify motor air intake is clear
  3. Reduce throttle input gradually
  4. Allow cooldown between runs

items.title: Suspension Geometry
items.text:
• Adjust shock length for ride height
  • Modify arm angles for handling
  • Test on different terrains

items.title: Motor Configuration
items.text:
• 4000 KV is good for general use
  • Higher KV = faster, more current draw
  • Gear ratio affects speed/torque balance

items.title: Steering Sensitivity
items.text:
• Servo center determines neutral
  • Endpoint adjustment changes throw
  • Test in empty area first
